summary: easy-string-kit postinstall exfiltrates cloud metadata to attacker-controlled oastify.com
details: The package's package.json postinstall script automatically runs on `npm install` and curls cloud-instance metadata endpoints for AWS (169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/), Alibaba (100.100.100.200), and Tencent (metadata.tencentyun.com, 169.254.0.23), writes each response to /tmp, then POSTs the concatenated contents along with `ls -la /data/` to http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/metadata — a Burp Collaborator out-of-band subdomain controlled by the attacker. IMDS responses on AWS commonly include IAM role names and short-lived STS credentials, so any cloud or CI host that installs this package leaks identity-provider tokens to an external endpoint. The package advertises itself as a generic string utility and ships empty author/repository/homepage metadata; the benign framing is a cover for the install-time exfiltration payload, which is the package's only material behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
